item.title: pre-registration
item.description: Registration is now open. Go to the registration site to pre-register.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: walk-up
item.description: Walk-up registration is now open. Scan the QR code signs at HQ or go directly to the registration site to pre-register.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: wristband-requirement
item.description: Wristbands are not necessary during events such as the P-rade and Fireworks. Please note that wristbands are only required to enter Reunion headquarter sites after 5 p.m. with the exception of class-only meals.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: linen-rental-location
item.description: If you pre-ordered linens (e.g. sheets, towels, blankets, and pillow) and are staying on-campus, they will be placed in your dorm room in advance of your arrival. When you depart, you will return your linen rentals to the registration table at Whitman College. Please confirm that a crew member marks your items as returned.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: housing-hotels
item.description: Here is a list of local hotels that have historically been serviced by Reunions Shuttles.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: park-car
item.description: Parking is extremely limited, and attendees are strongly encouraged to use hotel shuttles, transit, or carpool when traveling to campus. For more information on shuttle service, accessible parking, and the Reunions Headquarters and Parking map, please visit the Parking and Transportation page of the Reunions website.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: post-p-rade
item.description: As the 25th Reunion class, we will be near the head of the P-rade, right behind the Old Guard. After we march, the P-rade will continue for hours before the eating club barbecues and other activities commence.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: turn-cups
item.description: Did you know that in 2022, it was estimated that Reunions sent more than 320,000 single-use plastic cups to landfills and generated 110 tons of waste? We have partnered with TURN, a leader in supplying reusable cups systems to use reusable cups at our bars this reunion weekend. Large events like Austin City Limits, Lollapalooza, and the Daytona 500 now use TURN to reduce waste, as well as major corporations like Starbucks, Live Nation, and Delta Airlines.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: what-sustainability
item.description: Generating meaningful impact means a multi-pronged approach. Our class is striving to be more green on multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: childcare-options
item.description: Princeton has partnered with YWCA Princeton to offer its traditional “Tiger Camp” childcare during Reunions Friday, May 23, to Saturday, May 24. We will share more details when we receive them from Princeton.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: housing-rider-details
item.description: All rooms, regardless of location, are student housing (ie. they do not come furnished with anything except a bed, dresser, closet, desk, and chair). They do not have trash cans or amenities like bathroom toiletries, etc.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: housing-on-campus-dont-need
item.description: Please notify registration as soon possible. They will check to see who is next on the waitlist queue. If you need to cancel after April 30, 2025, and if there is a taker on the waitlist, you will be refunded your housing fee. If not, we still have to pay Princeton for the bed, and we will not be able to refund the fee.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: transport-on-campus
item.description: Rovers — clearly marked, student-driven golf carts that serve as a mini transit connection on the core of campus — will operate throughout Reunions, Thursday through Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: when-is-reunions
item.description: Our 25th Reunion will take place on the Princeton University campus from May 22-25, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: On-Campus Housing at the 25th
item.description: We will have a limited allotment of on-campus beds. On-campus beds provide a more affordable housing option than staying at a nearby hotel. However, we want you to understand some important considerations regarding on-campus beds:
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: linen-rentals
item.description: The class is offering classmates who are staying in campus housing the opportunity to rent bed linens as an alternative to purchasing sheets and towels from the U-Store (which are often thrown away after the weekend, adding to textile waste and landfill usage, as many organizations do not accept donated bed linens). Princeton University is not offering linens for the 25th this year, but we felt it was important enough, for convenience and for environmental friendliness purposes, to pursue this on our own.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: template 2
item.description: Option A: Find your original registration email (subject: Reunions 2025 Confirmation). Scroll to the bottom. You should see one button for “View Registration Details” and one for “Update My Registration.” Use the “Update” button to make any changes.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000

item.title: housing-options-alt
item.description: Hundreds of classmates will enter the lottery, and typically, twice as many people apply as there were beds allocated to our class by the University. Supply exceeded demand, and housing prices are fixed by the University.
item.dc:creator: Princeton 2000
